prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens in a pale blue clinical-looking office or examination room. A younger man sits in a chair across a desk from an older man in a white shirt and tie. The room contains medical-looking furniture, an examination table, cabinets, framed certificates, and wall-mounted images that resemble X-rays.

The camera cuts closer to the younger man, seated facing forward at the desk. A glass of water sits in front of him. He looks serious and still. The view then cuts to the older man behind the desk, with papers, a pen, and office items spread out in front of him. He appears to be addressing the younger man, though no speech is visually indicated. The camera returns to a tighter close-up of the younger man, whose expression looks tense or uncertain.

The setting then shifts abruptly to a blue-tinted car interior. The same younger man is now seated in the driver’s seat, smiling broadly. A quick close-up shows a printed page or document with a Mercedes-Benz logo at the top and a structured layout of text and boxes, though most of the text is not readable.

A sequence of driving and lifestyle images follows. A silver Mercedes-Benz sedan is shown driving through a city street lined with tall buildings. The man is seen again inside the car, hands on the steering wheel, looking relaxed and pleased. Another low-angle city shot shows the car passing along a curb while pedestrians walk on the sidewalk. A distant skyline appears across water under a clear sky.

The montage continues with leisure and travel imagery: people near water, a person stepping or jumping from a dock, and a shot involving the Golden Gate Bridge with the car and driver visible in the foreground. The silver sedan is shown parked beside a waterfront while the man, in a suit, stands on top of the car roof holding something to his face, possibly binoculars or a camera, though the object is not fully clear. The car then drives across a large steel bridge.

The visuals shift again to a beach or coastal setting. The same man appears bare-shouldered, smiling while moving along a shoreline. A wider shot shows dunes, grasses, and the ocean. The silver Mercedes sedan is then shown driving on an open road or bridge under a bright blue sky.

Near the end, the man appears again inside the car, now bare-shouldered, looking forward with a calmer expression. The commercial ends on a black title card with the Mercedes-Benz three-pointed star logo, the text “Mercedes-Benz,” and the large line “The C-Class starts at $29,950.” A website address appears at the top, and small legal fine print runs along the bottom, but most of the fine print is too small to read clearly.
